Tech giants' massive investments in AI capabilities create competition for limited computing resources, labor, and electricity. These cost pressures ripple through hardware, cloud services, and energy markets, ultimately raising prices for businesses and consumers.

title: 现在很多技术活，是不是已经开始被 AI 吃掉一部分了？
content: AI is increasingly replacing execution-focused technical tasks like building internal tools, front-end pages, data imports, and testing. While imperfect, AI handles "good enough" work that prioritizes functionality over elegant code. The author predicts this trend will compress roles in frontend, testing, design, and some backend work, especially for well-defined, describable tasks with clear acceptance criteria. Technical value will shift upstream toward business understanding, requirements decomposition, risk judgment, designing validation standards, and managing AI output quality. Adoption in China may lag due to workflow integration challenges, but the direction appears irreversible.

content: GitHub's remote-first culture, where employees primarily use handles rather than legal names, made transitioning easier for the author than at previous IT jobs that relied on real names. The company covers gender-affirming healthcare benefits including voice training, HRT, and therapy. Remote work eliminates concerns about office attire and public visibility, while asynchronous written communication reduced pressure during voice changes. After updating their name and pronouns internally, colleagues respected the transition without difficulty, offering genuine support.

title: 超12万人被裁，AI“血洗”科技圈
content: Global tech layoffs surpassed 120,000 in the first half of 2026, nearly matching 2025's total, with AI cited as the primary driver. Major companies including Oracle, Microsoft, Google, Meta, and Amazon cut thousands of roles despite record revenues, redirecting resources toward AI infrastructure and talent. Unlike previous cost-cutting waves, 2026's restructuring reflects strategic "cage-swapping": eliminating repetitive positions in HR, customer service, and middle management while expanding AI-focused hiring. Chinese tech firms like ByteDance and Alibaba similarly deny mass layoffs but quietly restructure around AI priorities. Industry experts warn some companies exploit "AI-washing" to disguise routine downsizing.

title: 经常对孩子说「这个太贵了，咱们网上买」，会咋样？真相和你想的不一样 | 原文
content: Telling children "it's too expensive, let's buy it online" is generally acceptable as it teaches price comparison and value awareness. However, frequently doing so may risk making children feel they don't deserve nice things. The article clarifies the phrase itself isn't harmful, but three related statements could foster feelings of scarcity and low self-worth in children.

title: 鸡蛋越来越大，这样的蛋一天生一个，一半以上的母鸡都生到骨折 | 原文
content: Commercial egg-laying hens have been selectively bred to produce increasingly large eggs daily, with Chinese market standards classing large eggs at 58+ grams and extra-large at 68+ grams. This intensive breeding causes severe welfare problems: over half of these hens suffer bone fractures due to calcium depletion from constant egg production, and many experience prolapse and other painful conditions. The industry's focus on maximizing egg size and output prioritizes productivity over animal welfare, subjecting birds to chronic suffering throughout their productive lives.

title: GPT-5.6紧急叫停，OpenAI最强模型被迫「一客一审」
content: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 release has been halted due to cybersecurity concerns, with the Trump administration requiring staggered deployment. Unlike past rapid launches, access now requires individual customer approval, marking an unprecedented restriction in AI release history. The model demonstrates advanced capabilities including 1.5M token context windows, improved reasoning, and strong frontend/UI generation. Meanwhile, Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 were withdrawn due to export controls, and Google's Gemini 3.5 Pro was delayed to July for quality improvements. All three flagship models missed their June release targets.

title: 中产最烧钱的户外运动：种地
content: Shared vegetable plots have become a prized escape for China's urban middle class, offering tangible fulfillment unavailable in abstract office work. Rentals cost 9.9 to 4,800 yuan yearly, plus equipment, labor, and travel expenses. Despite mosquitoes, distant locations, regulatory gray zones, and risks of prepaid scams, workers crave farming's reliable cause-and-effect loop. Tending land provides visible project completion, physical exercise, and psychological relief from meaningless corporate tasks—making a small plot represent freedom within crushing urban constraints.

title: 1 秒净赚 2.64 万，比英伟达还暴利，你相信「光」吗？
content: AI-driven demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) has transformed Micron from a cyclical storage chip maker into an extraordinarily profitable company, posting a record 84.9% gross margin and $28.24 billion quarterly net profit. Memory producers are redirecting capacity toward lucrative HBM, causing severe shortages and price surges in standard DRAM and NAND. This has cascaded through electronics markets: smartphone shipments face 10-13% cuts, manufacturers are reducing memory configurations and raising prices, while game consoles face delayed launches and reduced sales forecasts. Even Apple CEO Tim Cook called the cost inflation unprecedented, with iPhone 18 Pro hardware costs projected to rise 25%.

title: 突发：苹果深夜大涨价，黄牛疯狂囤货，iPhone 18危险了
content: Apple abruptly raised global prices for Mac, iPad, and home devices on June 25 due to soaring memory and storage costs driven by AI data center demand. MacBook prices jumped 17–20%, while low-memory models sold out immediately as scalpers hoarded inventory. Though iPhone prices remain unchanged temporarily, analysts expect iPhone 18 to follow suit later. The entire consumer electronics industry faces similar pressure, with memory prices up 160–300% since early 2025. Apple may alter future chip roadmaps and configurations to manage costs. Consumers face limited alternatives as competitors are also raising prices.

title: Evaluating performance and efficiency of the GitHub Copilot agentic harness across models and tasks
content: GitHub's agentic harness, shared across Copilot CLI, app, and code review, orchestrates tools and context for AI-powered development. Benchmarked against model-vendor harnesses (Claude Code, Codex CLI) using identical models and tasks, Copilot achieves comparable task-completion rates while consuming fewer tokens. Tests covered SWE-bench, TerminalBench, and internal benchmarks across GPT and Claude models. The harness supports 20+ models with auto-selection, enabling developers to balance cost and capability. Multi-model architecture enables unique features like cross-model critique for quality improvement.

title: Anthropic 指控阿里巴巴实施大规模 AI 模型蒸馏攻击
content: Anthropic accused Alibaba of conducting its largest-known model distillation attack. In a letter to a U.S. Senate committee, the AI company stated that Alibaba-linked operators used approximately 25,000 fraudulent accounts to interact with Anthropic models 28.8 million times between April 22 and June 5. Anthropic described the Chinese tech company's actions as "brazen" and "unlawful" attempts to steal AI capabilities.

title: 阿里巴巴证实：已起诉美国防部

title: Claude Code 升级为 Claude Tag，定位为更主动、擅长团队协作的企业协作工具
content: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, an enterprise collaboration tool upgrading Claude Code with proactive, team-focused AI assistance. Unlike personal AI assistants, it operates as a shared team member with continuous memory, shared context, proactive interventions, and asynchronous task execution. Currently paired with Opus 4.8 and available in Slack beta for Enterprise and Team users, it replaces the previous Slack integration within 30 days, with broader platform expansion planned.

title: 谷歌再失 AI 人才，两名 Gemini 核心骨干转投 Anthropic

They follow other prominent departures including Noam Shazeer to OpenAI and DeepMind director John Jumper to Anthropic. Rising talent outflows concern Google as rivals OpenAI and Anthropic leverage pre-IPO equity incentives to attract top AI researchers, a trend likely to continue.

title: IBM 发布全球首款亚纳米芯片技术
content: IBM has unveiled the world's first sub-nanometer chip technology, a 0.7-nanometer process using a 3D vertical stacking transistor architecture. The technology integrates nearly 100 billion transistors on a fingernail-sized chip, doubling the transistor density of its 2021 2-nanometer chip. Compared to 2-nanometer chips, it offers up to 50% performance improvement or 70% energy reduction, while reducing SRAM area by 40%.

title: OpenAI 再次升级 GPT-5.5 Instant AI：更具洞察力，购物推荐更实用
content: OpenAI upgraded its GPT-5.5 Instant model on June 25. The update improves intent understanding, reliability on complex tasks, and output quality with more natural, readable, and structured responses. It reduces verbose lists, stabilizes performance under complex constraints, and enhances shopping and local recommendations. The model is available to paying users immediately and free users starting June 26.

title: Prediction markets just posted their third straight record-setting week
content: Prediction markets set a third consecutive weekly record, with volume reaching $14.4 billion—up from $5-6 billion at the year's start and more than 10x year-over-year. Open interest hit $1.6 billion, also a third straight record, rising roughly 8x since last fall. World Cup activity contributed, but non-sports categories (politics, economics, geopolitics) drove major growth, hitting $3.6 billion on Kalshi and Polymarket combined—up from about $200 million in July 2025.
